Title: Verify Real-Time Localization of Commercial Activities
Preconditions: User is logged in and has location services enabled
Test Data: User’s current location within the app’s defined neighborhood boundaries
Test Steps:
1. Open the app and navigate to the map view.
2. Ensure the user’s current location is marked on the map.
3. Search for nearby commercial activities (e.g., shops, professionals).
4. Verify that the searched activities are displayed on the map with correct markers.
Expected Result: The map displays the user’s location and nearby commercial activities accurately, with markers labeled correctly.

Title: Verify GPS-Based Localization Accuracy
Preconditions: User has GPS enabled and is within the app’s neighborhood boundaries
Test Data: User’s known physical location (e.g., home address)
Test Steps:
1. Open the app and navigate to the map view.
2. Allow the app to access GPS location.
3. Confirm the app’s displayed location matches the user’s known physical location.
Expected Result: The app accurately reflects the user’s GPS-based location on the map.

Title: Verify Instant Messaging Functionality Between Users
Preconditions: Two users are registered and logged in
Test Data: Two test user accounts (User A and User B)
Test Steps:
1. User A sends a text message to User B via the app’s messaging feature.
2. User B opens the app and navigates to the messaging section.
3. User B reads the message sent by User A.
4. User B replies to User A’s message.
Expected Result: Messages are successfully sent, received, and displayed in real-time between the two users.

Title: Verify Unsold Goods Sharing Functionality
Preconditions: User is logged in as a commercial activity owner
Test Data: Sample unsold goods (e.g., expired products, surplus items)
Test Steps:
1. User posts an item for sharing (e.g., "Free: 5 kg of rice").
2. Another user searches for shared items and finds the posted item.
3. The second user requests the item.
4. The owner confirms the request.
Expected Result: The item is listed, searched, requested, and confirmed successfully.

Title: Verify Group Purchase Management
Preconditions: Multiple users are logged in
Test Data: Sample group purchase (e.g., bulk purchase of vegetables)
Test Steps:
1. User A creates a group purchase request (e.g., "Group buy: 20 kg of potatoes").
2. User B and User C join the group purchase.
3. User A finalizes the purchase and shares the details with participants.
Expected Result: The group purchase is created, joined by users, and finalized without errors.

Title: Verify Appointment Scheduling for Local Services
Preconditions: User is logged in as a professional/service provider
Test Steps:
1. User A (professional) posts an available appointment slot.
2. User B searches for available appointments and selects the slot.
3. User A confirms the booking.
Expected Result: The appointment is posted, searched, selected, and confirmed successfully.

Title: Verify Tool/Equipment Sharing Functionality
Preconditions: User is logged in and owns a tool/equipment
Test Steps:
1. User A lists a tool for sharing (e.g., "Ladder available for rent").
2. User B searches for shared tools and finds the listed tool.
3. User B requests to borrow the tool.
4. User A approves the request.
Expected Result: The tool is listed, searched, requested, and approved successfully.

Title: Verify User Registration with Residency Validation (if implemented)
Preconditions: User has valid residency documents (if required)
Test Data: Valid residency proof (e.g., utility bill, ID)
Test Steps:
1. User attempts to register with residency proof.
2. The app validates the residency document.
3. User completes registration.
Expected Result: The app accepts the residency proof and completes registration successfully.

Title: Verify Cross-Platform Compatibility for Android and iOS
Preconditions: Flutter SDK installed, Android Studio/XCode configured
Test Steps:
1. Build the app using Flutter for Android.
2. Install the APK on an Android device.
3. Launch the app and verify it loads without crashes.
4. Repeat steps 1–3 for iOS using Xcode and an iOS device.
Expected Result: App launches successfully on both Android and iOS devices without errors.

Title: Validate Real-Time Data Exchange Between Devices
Preconditions: Two devices (Android/iOS) with the app installed, active internet connection
Test Data: Sample data (e.g., user profile, location updates)
Test Steps:
1. Log in as two different users on Device A and Device B.
2. Update data (e.g., location, status) on Device A.
3. Verify Device B reflects the updated data within 5 seconds.
4. Repeat with Device B initiating changes.
Expected Result: Data synchronizes in real-time across both devices without delays or conflicts.

Title: Verify Role-Based Access for "Abitante" User
Preconditions: App installed, test account for "abitante" role created
Test Steps:
1. Log in with "abitante" credentials.
2. Navigate to all available menus/interfaces.
3. Attempt actions restricted to "professionista" (e.g., data entry, admin functions).
Expected Result: Only "abitante"-specific features are accessible; restricted actions are blocked.

Title: Verify Role-Based Access for "Professionista/Esercente" User
Preconditions: App installed, test account for "professionista" role created
Test Steps:
1. Log in with "professionista" credentials.
2. Navigate to all available menus/interfaces.
3. Attempt actions restricted to "abitante" (e.g., read-only views).
Expected Result: Only "professionista"-specific features are accessible; restricted actions are blocked.

Title: Validate Quarter-Level Activity Confinement
Preconditions: App installed, geolocation enabled, user logged in
Test Steps:
1. Log in and verify the app displays only data relevant to "Quarter X."
2. Attempt to access data from another quarter (e.g., via search or map).
3. Confirm no data from outside "Quarter X" is visible.
Expected Result: App restricts all actions/data to the user’s assigned quarter.

Title: Test Nominative Search Functionality
Preconditions: App installed, sample user data populated
Test Data: Valid username/ID of an existing user
Test Steps:
1. Log in as an "abitante" or "professionista."
2. Use the search function to input the username/ID.
3. Verify the correct user profile/data is displayed.
Expected Result: Search returns accurate, relevant results for the input query.

Title: Test Geographical Search Functionality
Preconditions: App installed, sample location-based data populated
Test Data: Valid geographical query (e.g., "near school," "in Quarter Y")
Test Steps:
1. Log in and access the search/map feature.
2. Input a geographical query.
3. Verify results are filtered to matching locations within the user’s quarter.
Expected Result: Search returns location-specific data without cross-quarter results.

Title: Verify Accessibility for Users with Language Barriers
Preconditions: App installed, language settings configured
Test Steps:
1. Change app language to a non-native language (e.g., English for Italian user).
2. Navigate through all primary interfaces (login, dashboard, search).
3. Confirm all UI elements (buttons, labels, errors) are translated.
Expected Result: App displays content in the selected language without missing translations.

Title: Validate Mandatory Internet Connectivity
Preconditions: App installed, device with toggleable internet
Test Steps:
1. Launch the app with active internet.
2. Disable internet and attempt to perform a data-dependent action (e.g., search, update).
3. Re-enable internet and retry the action.
Expected Result: App blocks actions requiring internet; error message appears. Action succeeds after reconnection.

Title: Validate Mandatory Geolocation
Preconditions: App installed, device with toggleable geolocation
Test Steps:
1. Launch the app with geolocation enabled.
2. Disable geolocation and attempt to access quarter-specific features.
3. Re-enable geolocation and retry.
Expected Result: App blocks geolocation-dependent features; error message appears. Features work after re-enabling.

Title: Verify Exception Handling for Technical Failures
Preconditions: App installed, test environment with forced errors (e.g., mock API failures)
Test Steps:
1. Simulate a backend/API failure (e.g., timeout, 500 error).
2. Attempt an action requiring backend communication (e.g., data update).
3. Observe app behavior (crash, freeze, or graceful degradation).
Expected Result: App displays user-friendly error message; no crash or data corruption occurs.

Title: Validate Demo Functionality Scope
Preconditions: Demo version of the app installed
Test Steps:
1. Launch the demo version.
2. Attempt to use all available features (limited to demo scope).
3. Attempt to access features not included in the demo (e.g., full database, sensors).
Expected Result: Demo works as specified; restricted features are inaccessible or grayed out.

Title: Verify Sensor Integration in Final Version
Preconditions: Final app version installed, device with active sensors (e.g., GPS, accelerometer)
Test Steps:
1. Launch the app and grant sensor permissions.
2. Perform an action requiring sensor input (e.g., location tracking, motion detection).
3. Verify sensor data is accurately captured and displayed.
Expected Result: App integrates sensor data correctly without errors or delays.

Title: Verify IDE Compatibility for Flutter Development
Preconditions: Android Studio, VSCode, or XCode with Flutter/Dart plugins installed on a test machine.
Test Steps:
1. Open Android Studio/VSCode/XCode with Flutter/Dart plugins.
2. Create a new Flutter project.
3. Verify the project structure matches Flutter conventions (e.g., `lib/`, `pubspec.yaml`).
4. Run the default Flutter app on an emulator or connected Android device (6.0+).
Expected Result: Project initializes successfully, app compiles and runs without errors, and emulator/device displays the default Flutter screen.

Title: Verify Firebase Integration for Data Persistence
Preconditions: Firebase project set up with a database enabled, Flutter app linked to Firebase.
Test Steps:
1. Add Firebase SDK to the Flutter project (`pubspec.yaml`).
2. Initialize Firebase in the app’s main Dart file.
3. Write a function to save sample data to Firebase Realtime Database/Firestore.
4. Retrieve the saved data and display it in the app UI.
Expected Result: Data is successfully saved and retrieved without errors, and the UI reflects the retrieved data.

Title: Verify Minimum Android Device Compatibility
Preconditions: Android device with OS 6.0+, 1GB RAM, 100MB storage, GPS, and Google Play Services 11.0+.
Test Steps:
1. Install the Flutter-built APK on the test device.
2. Open the app and verify it launches without crashes.
3. Test basic app functionality (e.g., UI rendering, button clicks).
4. Enable GPS and check if location-based features (if any) work.
Expected Result: App installs and runs smoothly, UI is responsive, and location services (if used) function as expected.

Title: Verify Adherence to OOP and Modularity Standards
Preconditions: Flutter project with multiple Dart files implementing features.
Test Data: Code snippets violating OOP principles (e.g., no encapsulation, redundant code).
Test Steps:
1. Review a Dart file for adherence to OOP (e.g., classes with private fields, inheritance, polymorphism).
2. Check for modularity (e.g., separate files for UI, logic, and data models).
3. Verify variable names are descriptive (e.g., `userName` instead of `un`).
4. Confirm comments explain non-trivial logic.
Expected Result: Code follows OOP principles, is modular, uses clear naming, and includes necessary comments.

Title: Verify Branch and Commit Workflow
Preconditions: GitHub repository with a `main` branch, local Flutter project cloned.
Test Data: Modified Dart file (e.g., updated UI widget).
Test Steps:
1. Create a new branch (`git checkout -b feature/new-ui`).
2. Make changes to a Dart file and stage them (`git add .`).
3. Commit changes with a descriptive message (`git commit -m "Updated login screen"`).
4. Push the branch to GitHub (`git push origin feature/new-ui`).
5. Create a pull request (PR) from the branch to `main`.
Expected Result: Branch is created, changes are committed with a clear message, pushed successfully, and PR is created without errors.

Title: Verify Integration Test Execution
Preconditions: Flutter project with at least two integrated modules (e.g., UI + Firebase).
Test Data: Test script covering UI-Firebase interaction (e.g., login flow).
Test Steps:
1. Write an integration test in Flutter’s `integration_test` package.
2. Test a user flow (e.g., login → navigate to dashboard).
3. Verify data is saved/retrieved from Firebase during the flow.
4. Run the test (`flutter test integration_test/login_flow_test.dart`).
Expected Result: Test passes without failures, and the app behaves as expected during the tested flow.

Title: Verify Backup and Recovery Process
Preconditions: Flutter project with uncommitted changes, local and remote backups configured.
Test Steps:
1. Delete the uncommitted file locally.
2. Restore the file from a local backup (e.g., `git checkout HEAD -- path/to/file`).
3. Push the restored file to GitHub (`git push origin main`).
4. Verify the file matches the backup version.
Expected Result: File is successfully restored from backup, and the remote repository reflects the correct version.

Title: Verify Team Collaboration Tools
Preconditions: WhatsApp group, Google Drive folder, and GitHub repository shared with team members.
Test Steps:
1. Upload `design_specs.pdf` to Google Drive and share the link in WhatsApp.
2. Commit a code snippet to a GitHub branch and notify the team via WhatsApp.
3. Verify all team members can access the document and code changes.
Expected Result: Document and code are accessible to all team members, and notifications are received without delay.

Title: Verify GPS and Google Play Services Compatibility
Preconditions: Android device with GPS and Google Play Services 11.0+ enabled.
Test Steps:
1. Open the app and navigate to a feature requiring location (e.g., map view).
2. Enable GPS on the device.
3. Verify the app requests location permissions and displays accurate location data.
Expected Result: App requests permissions correctly, GPS data is fetched, and location is displayed without errors.

Title: User Registration Validation
Preconditions: User has opened the registration screen.
Test Data: Valid email (e.g., test@example.com), valid password (8+ chars), valid name.
Test Steps:
1. Enter valid email, name, and password in the registration form.
2. Click the "Register" button.
Expected Result: User account is created successfully in Firebase Authentication, and a confirmation message is displayed.

Title: User Login Validation
Preconditions: User has opened the login screen.
Test Steps:
1. Enter registered email and correct password in the login form.
2. Click the "Login" button.
Expected Result: User is logged in successfully, and the app navigates to the home screen.

Title: Invalid User Login Attempt
Preconditions: User has opened the login screen.
Test Steps:
1. Enter registered email and incorrect password in the login form.
2. Click the "Login" button.
Expected Result: Error message "Invalid email or password" is displayed.

Title: User Data Persistence Test
Preconditions: User is logged in.
Test Data: Sample user profile data (e.g., name, bio).
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the profile section.
2. Update user profile data (e.g., name, bio).
3. Log out and log back in.
Expected Result: Updated user profile data is retained and displayed correctly.

Title: Real-Time Messaging Functionality
Preconditions: Two users are logged in and in the same chat.
Test Steps:
1. User A sends a message in the chat.
2. User B refreshes the chat screen.
Expected Result: User B sees the message sent by User A in real-time.

Title: Notification for New Messages
Preconditions: User A is logged in, User B is logged out.
Test Steps:
1. User A sends a message to User B.
2. User B logs back in.
Expected Result: User B receives a notification indicating a new message.

Title: Post Creation and Display
Preconditions: User is logged in.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the post creation screen.
2. Enter post content and click "Post."
3. Navigate to the feed screen.
Expected Result: The newly created post appears in the user's feed.

Title: Interaction with Local Activities
Preconditions: User is logged in and has access to local activities.
Test Data: Sample activity data (e.g., event name, date, location).
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the local activities section.
2. Select an activity and click "Join."
Expected Result: User is marked as participating in the activity, and a confirmation is displayed.

Test Steps:
1. Open the task scheduling module.
2. Locate task K.2 in the list.
3. Verify the duration is set to 5 working days.
4. Check the deadlines are correctly listed as 11/11/2022, 17/11/2022, 05/12/2022, and 9/12/2022.
Expected Result: Task K.2 displays the correct duration and deadlines.

Preconditions: Task L.2 is selected for review.
Test Data: Task L.2 depends on L.1, duration: 8 working days, deadlines: 25/10/2022, 04/11/2022, 24/11/2022, 5/12/2022
Test Steps:
1. Open the task scheduling module.
2. Locate task L.2 in the list.
3. Verify the dependency is correctly set to L.1.
4. Check the duration is set to 8 working days.
5. Confirm deadlines are listed as 25/10/2022, 04/11/2022, 24/11/2022, and 5/12/2022.
Expected Result: Task L.2 shows the correct dependency, duration, and deadlines.

Title: Verify Task Duration and Deadlines for M.1
Preconditions: Task M.1 is selected for review.
Test Data: Task M.1 depends on L.3 and K.1, duration: 3 working days, deadlines: 11/11/2022, 15/11/2022, 12/12/2022, 22/12/2022
Test Steps:
1. Open the task scheduling module.
2. Locate task M.1 in the list.
3. Verify dependencies are correctly set to L.3 and K.1.
4. Check the duration is set to 3 working days.
5. Confirm deadlines are listed as 11/11/2022, 15/11/2022, 12/12/2022, and 22/12/2022.
Expected Result: Task M.1 displays the correct dependencies, duration, and deadlines.

Title: Verify Task Duration and Deadlines for M.3
Preconditions: Task M.3 is selected for review.
Test Data: Task M.3 depends on L.1, L.4, and K.4, duration: 10 working days, deadlines: 18/11/2022, 01/12/2022, 12/12/2022, 22/12/2022
Test Steps:

2. Locate task M.3 in the list.
3. Verify dependencies are correctly set to L.1, L.4, and K.4.

Title: Verify Budget Allocation for Human Resources
Preconditions: Budget allocation module is open.
Test Data: Human resources budget: €600/week, total €7,200 for 12 weeks (4 members  2 hours/day  15€/hour)
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the budget allocation section.
2. Locate the "GRUPPO" category under "SETTORE."
3. Verify the total budget for human resources is €7,200.
4. Confirm the breakdown is €600 per week for 12 weeks.
Expected Result: The budget allocation for human resources matches the provided values.

Title: Verify Budget Allocation for Functionalities
Preconditions: Budget allocation module is open.
Test Data: Functionalities budget breakdown: Business logic €1,600, Database dinamico €400, Messaggistica €400, Mappatura attività commerciali €400, Extra €300, total €3,600
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the budget allocation section.
2. Locate the "Funzionalità implementate" category under "SETTORE."
3. Verify the individual budget allocations: Business logic €1,600, Database dinamico €400, Messaggistica €400, Mappatura attività commerciali €400, Extra €300.
4. Confirm the total budget for functionalities is €3,600.
Expected Result: The budget allocation for functionalities matches the provided values.

Title: Verify Total Budget Calculation
Preconditions: Budget allocation module is open.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the budget allocation section.
2. Sum the partial totals: Human resources €7,200, Documentazione €2,150, Funzionalità implementate €3,600, Grafica €500.
3. Verify the total budget is €13,450.
Expected Result: The total budget matches the provided value of €13,450.

Title: Verify Project Deadlines
Preconditions: Project timeline module is open.
Test Data: Deadlines: Piano di Progetto (17/10/2022), Documento dei requisiti (31/10/2022), Piano di Testing (14/11/2022), Documento di progettazione (28/11/2022), Rilascio versione completa (15/12/2022), Versione integrata (15/01/2023)
Test Steps:
1. Open the project timeline module.
2. Locate each deadline in the list.
3. Verify the deadlines are correctly listed as: Piano di Progetto (17/10/2022), Documento dei requisiti (31/10/2022), Piano di Testing (14/11/2022), Documento di progettazione (28/11/2022), Rilascio versione completa (15/12/2022), Versione integrata (15/01/2023).
Expected Result: All deadlines are correctly displayed in the timeline module.

Title: Verify Software Tools Availability
Preconditions: Resource management module is open.
Test Data: Required software tools: VSCode with Flutter extensions, Git, Google Drive, Firebase API documentation, Android Studio, XCode
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the software resources section.
2. Verify the availability of VSCode with Flutter extensions.
3. Confirm Git is listed as a required tool.
4. Check that Google Drive is included for documentation sharing.
5. Verify Firebase API documentation is available.
6. Confirm Android Studio and XCode are listed for app export.
Expected Result: All required software tools are correctly listed and accessible.

Title: Verify Hardware Tools Availability
Preconditions: Resource management module is open.
Test Data: Required hardware tools: Macintosh system, Android and iOS devices for testing
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the hardware resources section.
2. Verify at least one Macintosh system is available.
3. Confirm Android and iOS devices are listed for testing.
Expected Result: All required hardware tools are correctly listed and available.
